figures.caption: Figure 1. 3D rendering of a representative BraTS-METS case. (a) Semantic reference label with four tumor sub-regions overlaid on the T1c brain volume. (b–e) Per-class instance decomposition via connected components: (b) SNFH (15 components), (c) ET (17 components), (d) RC (1 component), (e) NETC (4 components). Numbers index individual instances for each class.
Table 5. Foreground-mean panoptic quality (PQ) at three DSC thresholds τ . Higher is better. Best in bold, second-best underlined. †: β = 2.
Table 6. Foreground-mean recognition quality (RQ) at three DSC thresholds τ . Higher is better. Best in bold, second-best underlined. †: β = 2.

Figure 3. RC Dice vs. FG-Mean PQDSC 0.5 . Filled markers: β=1; hollow: β=2. Instance losses (stars) improve RC Dice over the baseline while preserving PQDSC 0.5 . IWL variants (triangles) further increase RC Dice at the cost of PQDSC 0.5 . Standalone inverse weighting (squares) shows low performance on both metrics.

Figure 2. Overview of instance-sensitive loss decomposition. (a) Gradient contribution by class (color) and instance size (hatch). DC+CE is dominated by large instances in common classes; inverse-size weighting shifts to small instances but RC remains underrepresented; Blob/CC with class decomposition equalizes both classes and instances; adding IWL within components further emphasizes small instances while preserving class balance. (b) CC loss partitions the volume into Voronoi cells V c k . (c) Blob loss pairs each component Sc k with the background.

Figure 4. Effect of β weighting on foreground-mean DSC and PQDSC 0.5 . Dashed line: baseline (DC+CE). β=1 (blue) consistently outperforms β=2 (red) across all methods, with the gap widening for IWL variants.
Table 2. Size-stratified foreground-mean PQDSC 0.5 by instance size (small <0.5 cc, medium 0.5–5 cc, large >5 cc). Best in bold, second-best underlined. †: β = 2.
Table 4. Region-wise PQDSC 0.5 on BraTS-METS 2025 (260 cases). Best in bold, second-best underlined. †: β = 2.

Table 3. Region-wise DSC on BraTS-METS 2025 (260 cases). WT = whole tumor, TC = tumor core, ET = enhancing tumor, RC = resection cavity, FG = foreground mean. Best in bold, second-best underlined. †: β = 2.
